name: soredium
description: Development workflow + knowledge garden skills for Claude Code

plugins.name: adr
plugins.description: Use when recording architectural decisions: user says "create an ADR", "document why we chose X", significant technic...

plugins.name: code-review
plugins.description: Use when the user says "review the code", "check these changes", "review this", "look at staged changes", or invokes ...

plugins.name: dependency-update
plugins.description: Use when the user says "bump a version", "upgrade dependencies", "check for newer versions", "add a dependency", or "...

plugins.name: forage
plugins.description: Use when non-obvious technical knowledge surfaces during session work — bugs whose symptoms mislead about root cause, tools that contradict documentation, silent failures with no error, workarounds found only via multiple failed approaches, techniques a skilled developer wouldn't naturally reach for.

plugins.name: git-commit
plugins.description: Use when user wants to create a commit NOW — says "commit this", "commit these changes", "create a commit". Routes to...

plugins.name: git-squash
plugins.description: Use when the commit history contains noise — fixup commits, revert chains, docs wording chores, tiny cleanups — that ...

plugins.name: handover
plugins.description: Use when ending a mid-work session (branch stays open) and wanting to preserve context for resumption, OR when a session is beginning and needing to resume from where things left off.

plugins.name: harvest
plugins.description: Use when deduplicating existing garden entries (DEDUPE) or reviewing all stale entries across the garden (REVIEW). Dedicated session only — do not use during normal session work; use forage for session-time operations.

plugins.name: ide-tooling
plugins.description: INVOKE IMMEDIATELY when mcp__intellij-index__* tools are visible. Use for ALL code navigation, editing, refactoring, and diagnostics. Also invoke for any IDE operation: rename, move, edit member, find references, type hierarchy, diagnostics.

plugins.name: idea-log
plugins.description: Use when user wants to park an idea for later — says "log this idea", "add to idea log", "park that thought", "we sho...

plugins.name: implementation-doc-sync
plugins.description: Use when documentation needs syncing — user says "sync docs", "update docs", "doc sweep", or invoked from a prompt sn...

plugins.name: issue-workflow
plugins.description: Use when setting up GitHub issue tracking, planning implementation work (creating epics and child issues from a plan)...

plugins.name: java-dev
plugins.description: Use when writing new Java code, fixing bugs, refactoring, or adding tests in Quarkus applications — user says "implem...

plugins.name: project-health
plugins.description: Use when correctness, completeness, or consistency of a project needs verification — "is the project healthy?", "pre-...

plugins.name: project
plugins.description: Use when project setup needs to be verified or completed — invoked automatically at session start (via hook) and by w...

plugins.name: project-refine
plugins.description: Use when seeking improvement opportunities in a project — "find duplication", "bloat check", "look for ways to improv...

plugins.name: protocol
plugins.description: Use when working with project-level protocol files — project-specific rules, architectural constraints, and conventions that apply within a single project.

plugins.name: publish-blog
plugins.description: Use when publishing blog entries to external platforms via blog-routing.yaml — user says "publish blog", "publish ent...

plugins.name: python-dev
plugins.description: Use when writing new Python code, fixing bugs, refactoring, or adding tests — user says "implement", "fix", "refactor...

plugins.name: retro-issues
plugins.description: Use when mapping an existing git repository's history to GitHub epics and issues — user says "map our history to issu...

plugins.name: security-audit
plugins.description: Use when user explicitly requests "security review", "audit security", "check for vulnerabilities", or "OWASP check",...

plugins.name: ts-dev
plugins.description: Use when writing new TypeScript code, fixing bugs, refactoring, or adding tests — user says "implement", "fix", "refa...

plugins.name: update-claude-md
plugins.description: Use when CLAUDE.md needs updating due to workflow or convention changes, or when invoked automatically by git-commit/...

plugins.name: update-design
plugins.description: Use when the user says "update the design doc", "sync ARC42STORIES.MD", "sync ARC42STORIES.MD", "reflect code changes in th...

plugins.name: work
plugins.description: Use when the user says "work", "work end", "work pause", or "work resume" — detects current branch state and routes t...

plugins.name: work-end
plugins.description: Use when the current branch is complete and ready to close — user says "work-end", "close this branch", or "wrap up t...

plugins.name: work-pause
plugins.description: Use when interrupting current branch work to switch to something else — user says "work-pause", "pause this work", or...

plugins.name: work-resume
plugins.description: Use when returning to a paused branch — user says "work-resume", "resume", or "go back to that branch". Reads the pau...

plugins.name: work-start
plugins.description: Use when a task or session is beginning — the opening directive in any work-item prompt, or when the user says they a...

plugins.name: workspace-init
plugins.description: Use when explicitly creating a new companion workspace from scratch — user invokes /workspace-init or says "init work...

plugins.name: write-content
plugins.description: Use when writing any piece of content — project diary entry, blog post, article, note, brief, README, news item, tech...

plugins.name: brainstorming
plugins.description: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.

plugins.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
plugins.description: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies

plugins.name: executing-plans
plugins.description: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints

plugins.name: receiving-code-review
plugins.description: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation

plugins.name: requesting-code-review
plugins.description: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements. Dispatches a fresh subagent for review — distinct from the session-level code-review skill which reviews staged changes using language-specific checklists.

plugins.name: subagent-driven-development
plugins.description: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session

plugins.name: systematic-debugging
plugins.description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes

plugins.name: test-driven-development
plugins.description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code

plugins.name: using-git-worktrees
plugins.description: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - ensures an isolated workspace exists via native tools or git worktree fallback

plugins.name: using-superpowers
plugins.description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring skill invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions

plugins.name: verification-before-completion
plugins.description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always

plugins.name: writing-plans
plugins.description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code

plugins.name: writing-skills
plugins.description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment

plugins.name: design-review
plugins.description: Use when a design spec needs adversarial review — user says "review this design", "design review", "tear this spec apart", "pre-review this", or invokes /design-review. Orchestrates two independent Claude sessions (reviewer + implementor) with a Python PM tracking every issue to evidence-based resolution.

plugins.name: fix-ci
plugins.description: Use when CI is red and the user asks to fix it, or when a push fails CI and the user asks "is CI green?" after a fix. Ensures all failures are reproduced locally, root-caused, and verified green before any push. Never use CI as a test runner.
